name: Bitcoin Basics
description: Core concepts behind Bitcoin and how it works.
author: OFS Project - Matthew Husak

back.value: A decentralised digital currency that operates without a central bank or single administrator, using peer-to-peer technology.
A public, append-only ledger of all Bitcoin transactions, stored as a chain of blocks. Each block references the previous one, making past records tamper-evident.

front.value: What is the maximum supply of Bitcoin?

back.value: 21 million BTC. This hard cap is enforced by the protocol and cannot be changed without consensus from the network.

The process by which new transactions are verified and added to the blockchain. Miners compete to solve a computational puzzle (Proof of Work), and the winner adds the next block and earns a block reward.
An event that cuts the block reward in half, occurring every 210,000 blocks (~4 years). It controls Bitcoin's issuance rate and is built into the protocol.
A secret 256-bit number that proves ownership of Bitcoin. Anyone with access to your private key can spend your funds. It should never be shared.

front.value: What does **"not your keys, not your coins"** mean?

back.value: If you don't hold your own private keys (e.g. your Bitcoin is held on an exchange), you don't truly own it — the custodian does.
A layer-2 payment protocol built on top of Bitcoin. It enables fast, low-fee transactions by routing payments through a network of off-chain payment channels.
